title: Reach behavioral parity with Rust Figment
description: Bring the TypeScript port to high-confidence behavioral parity with Rust Figment. This epic covers provenance/tag fidelity, multi-error behavior, value-layer parity, provider feature parity, and parity-oriented test infrastructure.
design:
Architecture goals:
- Keep domain-grouped structure (`src/core`, `src/providers`, `src/value` or equivalent) and avoid flattening modules.
- Preserve existing public APIs where possible; add parity APIs where missing.
- Prefer explicit semantic test fixtures over implicit behavior assumptions.
- Prioritize deterministic behavior and source attribution in all merge/extract/error paths.

System shape:
- Figment core remains the merge/coalesce coordinator.
- Provenance state (tags + metadata) is first-class and traversable by path and tag.
- Error aggregate model is first-class and iterable.
- Providers map source-native parsing behavior into canonical internal values with source metadata.
- Parity suite validates behavior against Rust semantics in representative fixtures.
acceptance_criteria:
## Epic Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All child workstreams are completed and linked under this epic.
- [ ] `doc/gaps.md` parity items are either closed or explicitly deferred with rationale.
- [ ] Provenance for winning keys is queryable and stable for nested paths.
- [ ] Multi-error behavior is deterministic and preserves context per error.
- [ ] Provider behavior parity is validated for JSON/TOML/YAML/Env edge cases, including nested profile behavior.
- [ ] Test suite includes parity fixtures that cover merge/join precedence, profile/global/default behavior, provenance, and chained errors.
- [ ] CI-level commands (`check`, `fix`, `build`) pass with the parity suite enabled.
- [ ] Documentation reflects parity guarantees and known intentional deviations (if any).

title: Implement Rust-like multi-error aggregation and context
description: Upgrade error semantics to preserve deterministic multi-error chains with rich path/profile/metadata/tag context and Rust-like display behavior.
design:
Implementation areas:
- `src/core/error.ts`: evolve `FigmentError` into an aggregate-friendly model.
- `src/figment.ts`: ensure provider and extraction failures append consistently to the same chain.
- formatting surface: deterministic text rendering with interpolated keys and source descriptors.

Design constraints:
- Preserve original error ordering rules (newest vs previous) intentionally and test it.
- Keep context immutable once attached to a chain node.
- Avoid swallowing upstream error details; wrap with context rather than replacing.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Error aggregate supports deterministic chaining and traversal.
- [ ] Aggregate can report count/length of contained errors.
- [ ] Per-error context includes path, profile, metadata, and tag where available.
- [ ] Provider parse/load failures and extraction failures both chain correctly.
- [ ] Display formatting includes kind/message + interpolated key + source/provider context.
- [ ] Multi-line error rendering is stable and test-assertable.
- [ ] Missing-field errors include full path segmentation context.
- [ ] Context attachment does not mutate prior chain nodes unexpectedly.
- [ ] Tests cover multiple-failure scenarios and ordering guarantees.
- [ ] README/docs include practical error-output examples.

title: Finalize metadata and tag provenance parity
description: Complete provenance propagation so every winning value can be traced to deterministic source metadata/tag context across coalescing and extraction paths.
design:
Implementation areas:
- `src/core/tag.ts`: represent tag trees robustly for dict/array/primitive nodes.
- `src/core/coalesce.ts`: ensure tag coalescing mirrors value coalescing exactly per operation (`join`, `merge`, `adjoin`, `admerge`).
- `src/core/path.ts`: reliable path traversal for both value and tag trees.
- `src/figment.ts`: tie provider metadata registration, tag assignment, merged-state tag lookup, and extraction/error context together.

Design constraints:
- No flat module sprawl; keep provenance code under `src/core/*` and orchestration in `src/figment.ts`.
- Provenance lookup must be deterministic for conflicting and nested keys.
- Avoid lossy conversion of tags during focus/select/merge operations.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every provider merge/join assigns stable tags to produced values.
- [ ] Tag coalescing semantics exactly mirror value coalescing for all 4 operations.
- [ ] `findMetadata(path)` returns metadata for the winning leaf value at that path.
- [ ] `getMetadata(tag)` returns registered metadata for valid tags and `undefined` otherwise.
- [ ] Nested dict conflicts preserve correct winning tag lineage at each nested key.
- [ ] Array behavior preserves tag lineage for concat (`ad*`) and replace/keep (`join`/`merge`) semantics.
- [ ] `focus(path)` preserves both value and tag shape for resulting subtree figments.
- [ ] Missing-path lookups never return stale/incorrect tags.
- [ ] Provenance behavior is covered by unit tests for merge-vs-join winners and nested conflicts.
- [ ] Documentation includes examples of path->metadata provenance lookups.

title: Complete provider feature parity and edge-case behavior
description: Reach provider-level behavior parity for Data/Env/Serialized and format variants, including edge-case parsing and metadata behavior.
design:
Implementation areas:
- `src/providers/data.ts`: nested profile parsing, file search/required semantics, metadata source behavior, YAML extended support.
- `src/providers/env.ts`: key mapping/filtering/splitting edge cases and TOML-ish value parsing nuances.
- `src/providers/serialized.ts`: keyed/unkeyed behavior and metadata fidelity.

Design constraints:
- Preserve source-native expectations while matching Rust semantics where intended.
- Keep provider APIs composable and chainable.
- Ensure provider metadata and tags integrate with core provenance model.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Data` file/string behavior matches expected profile/nested semantics.
- [ ] `Data.required()` and `Data.search()` behavior is parity-tested against missing/exact/parent-search scenarios.
- [ ] Source metadata for file providers reflects winning/actual source path expectations.
- [ ] `YamlExtended` behavior is implemented or explicitly deferred with documented rationale.
- [ ] `Env` transformations (`prefixed`, `split`, `only`, `ignore`, `map`, `filterMap`) are edge-case tested.
- [ ] `Env` value parsing behavior is deterministic and documented (including fallback rules).
- [ ] `Serialized` keyed/unkeyed paths preserve expected map/value behavior.
- [ ] Provider-specific interpolation behavior is validated in diagnostics.
- [ ] Provider outputs integrate correctly with provenance/tag lookups.
- [ ] Parity fixture tests cover representative Rust docs scenarios.

title: Establish parity fixture suite and verification workflow
description: Create a comprehensive parity-oriented test suite and repeatable workflow that validates TypeScript behavior against key Rust Figment semantics.
design:
Implementation areas:
- Build fixture-based tests for precedence, profiles, provenance, provider behavior, and errors.
- Organize tests by domain (coalesce, providers, provenance, errors) to mirror architecture.
- Add check/fix/build workflows that include parity suites and produce deterministic outputs.

Design constraints:
- Prefer explicit fixtures over ad-hoc inline behavior assumptions.
- Keep tests fast enough for default local runs while allowing deeper parity runs.
- Ensure test names clearly describe semantic expectations.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test directories are domain-grouped and map to core workstreams.
- [ ] Fixture coverage includes merge/join/adjoin/admerge conflict scenarios.
- [ ] Fixture coverage includes profile selection (`default`, `global`, custom profiles).
- [ ] Fixture coverage includes provenance lookups (`findMetadata`, tag-to-metadata paths).
- [ ] Fixture coverage includes multi-error chaining and formatting assertions.
- [ ] Fixture coverage includes provider edge cases (Env/Data/Serialized).
- [ ] `pnpm run check` executes parity-relevant checks successfully.
- [ ] `pnpm run fix` remains compatible with parity test setup.
- [ ] Test docs explain how to add new parity fixtures.
- [ ] CI/local workflow documentation identifies required commands and expected outcomes.

title: Build value-layer parity and magic-value foundations
description: Close the gap between the current generic TS value tree and Rust Figment value-layer capabilities required for richer extraction and provenance-aware behaviors.
design:
Implementation areas:
- Introduce a stronger internal value representation (or adapters) for typed distinctions needed by diagnostics and providers.
- Add provenance-aware value helpers analogous to Rust magic-value patterns.
- Ensure serialization/deserialization boundaries preserve information needed for errors and tags.

Design constraints:
- Keep public API ergonomic while preserving rich internal metadata.
- Avoid brittle type-casting paths that erase semantic distinctions.
- Keep compatibility with provider outputs and existing extraction APIs.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Internal value model can express required scalar/container distinctions for diagnostics.
- [ ] Tagged value access patterns are exposed or emulated in a stable API.
- [ ] Provenance-aware value helpers are documented and tested.
- [ ] Relative/source-aware value behavior has a clear TS equivalent design (or explicit documented deviation).
- [ ] Value conversion paths preserve tags and context through extraction boundaries.
- [ ] Lossy extraction rules are explicit and covered by tests.
- [ ] Tests verify no regression in existing provider composition behavior.
- [ ] Module layout remains domain-grouped (`src/value/*` or equivalent grouped design).
- [ ] Any intentional non-parity behavior is documented with rationale.
- [ ] Type declarations remain strict and pass typecheck under current ts config.
